title: T-080 — FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval API/actions
prompt:
# T-080 — FactorySpec+BOM bundle approval API/actions

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §0, §5.1A, §7.4, §7.6, prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md §0A
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Backend/action contract for atomic factory_specs + shared BOM approval bundle.

One task type: API/actions/service tests. UI panel/modal implementation is T-090. Approval starts from T-079 factory_specs and T-081 release adapter; local dependencies remain 03-TECHNICAL task IDs only. Settings T-122/T-123 authorization dependencies are declared under cross_module_dependencies.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- apps/web/actions/technical/release-bundles/*.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/technical/release-bundle-service.ts [create]
- tests/actions/technical/release-bundle.test.ts [create]
- tests/lib/technical/release-bundle-service.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given factory_spec is in_review and BOM is draft/reviewable with passing RM usability, when Technical approver approves bundle, then both move to canonical factory-usable status through T-081 and one audit/evidence bundle id links them.
2. Given BOM passes but factory_spec fails release blocker, then approval is rejected atomically and neither record is released.
3. Given D365 integration is disabled, local Technical release still works and D365 sync/export state remains optional integration metadata.
4. Given released bundle is edited, API creates next draft versions through clone-on-write and keeps released versions immutable.
5. Given user lacks Technical approval permission from Settings policy, approve/reject actions fail with explicit authorization error.

## Test strategy
- RED unit/action tests for atomic approve/reject and split prohibition.
- RED tests for Settings authorization preflight and D365-disabled local release.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/actions/technical/release-bundle.test.ts tests/lib/technical/release-bundle-service.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not create a second BOM model or NPD-owned factory_spec path.
- Do not release only one side of the bundle by accident.
- Do not require D365 to approve local Technical release.
- Do not mutate released versions in place.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
